text:
GENITOURINARY ONCOLOGY — FOLLOW-UP VISIT
Patient: Robert Delacroix | DOB: on file | MRN: on file
Attending: GU Oncology

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Return visit for follow-up on olaparib therapy, initiated approximately 8 weeks ago for castration-resistant prostate cancer with germline BRCA2 mutation. Also evaluating interval labs and new-onset fatigue.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Mr. Delacroix is a 71-year-old French-Canadian male with a well-characterized history of high-grade metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC). He was initially diagnosed approximately 3 years ago with Stage IVB (cT3bN1M1b) Gleason 9 (4+5) Grade Group 5 acinar adenocarcinoma with bone and pelvic lymph node metastases at presentation. PSA at diagnosis was 87.4 ng/mL. He was started on ADT with leuprolide plus abiraterone/prednisone (mCSPC setting), achieving excellent PSA nadir of 0.8 ng/mL. He transitioned to CRPC approximately 18 months into treatment with rising PSA (nadir 0.8 → 1.8 → 3.4 → 6.7 ng/mL over 3 months, confirmed castrate testosterone). At that time, importantly, germline BRCA2 testing had already revealed a pathogenic variant (c.5946delT, p.Ser1982Argfs*22) with confirmed somatic LOH, making him a clear PROfound-eligible candidate for PARP inhibition. However, enzalutamide was chosen as the next AR-targeted option given he had not yet had a second-generation ARSI in the castration-resistant setting, with plan to preserve olaparib.

He received enzalutamide 160 mg daily for approximately 9 months. He had an initial PSA response down to a nadir of 0.6 ng/mL, but over the subsequent 3 months developed biochemical and then radiographic progression — PSA rising to 9.2 ng/mL and a bone scan demonstrating new lesions in T10, T12, and right 4th rib. Spine MRI identified T10 epidural extension with approximately 35% canal compromise without cord signal change, for which he underwent palliative 3D-CRT to T10-T12 (20 Gy/5 fx) with good pain response (back pain improved from grade 2 to grade 1). Enzalutamide was discontinued. He was then started on olaparib (Lynparza) 300 mg BID approximately 8 weeks ago, with leuprolide continued for ongoing castration. At olaparib initiation, baseline hemoglobin was 11.8 g/dL, WBC 6.2, platelets 198.

He presents today for his first formal follow-up since starting olaparib. He reports that his back pain has continued to improve and is now grade 1 and manageable with scheduled acetaminophen. He is pleased that the PSA has declined significantly per his at-home review of the patient portal. However, he is concerned about worsening fatigue over the past 2-3 weeks, noting increased dyspnea with exertion such as climbing one flight of stairs, and some palpitations at rest. He denies bleeding (no hematochezia, melena, hematuria, or epistaxis). He denies fever, chills, or infectious symptoms. Nausea has been present, described as grade 1, intermittent, managed acceptably with ondansetron as needed. He denies vomiting, oral mucositis, or weight change perceived by him as significant. He has been compliant with all medications.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Positive: Fatigue (worsening, moderate), dyspnea on exertion (new, grade 1-2), palpitations (intermittent, at rest), nausea (grade 1, controlled), mild back pain (residual grade 1 from prior vertebral mets), hot flashes (chronic, grade 2-3, unchanged from prior enzalutamide era).
Negative: No fever, no chills, no night sweats, no headaches, no visual changes, no dizziness (improved from enzalutamide), no chest pain at rest, no abdominal pain, no diarrhea, no constipation, no hematuria, no hematochezia, no melena, no bleeding of any kind, no new bone pain, no neurologic symptoms, no confusion, no oral lesions.

---

PAST ONCOLOGIC HISTORY
- Stage IVB (cT3bN1M1b) Gleason 9 (4+5) Grade Group 5 prostatic acinar adenocarcinoma, diagnosed age 68
  - Bone mets (L2-L4, pelvis, right femur, ribs) and pelvic LN involvement at diagnosis
  - ADT (leuprolide) + abiraterone/prednisone: ~18 months, PSA nadir 0.8 ng/mL
  - Bone-protective: zoledronic acid (Zometa) 4 mg q3 months ongoing
  - Transitioned to CRPC, enzalutamide 160 mg x 9 months, PSA nadir 0.6 ng/mL then progression
  - New bone metastases T10, T12, right 4th rib; T10 epidural soft tissue extension
  - Palliative EBRT T10-T12 (20 Gy/5 fx), completed prior to current visit
  - Olaparib 300 mg BID initiated (PROfound indication, germline BRCA2)
- Germline: BRCA2 c.5946delT pathogenic variant; somatic LOH confirmed; genetic counseling provided
- NGS (biopsy): TP53 p.R248W, PTEN biallelic deletion, CDK12 frameshift, RB1 splice site, PIK3CA p.E545K; AR amplification (CN 8); MSS; TMB 4 mut/Mb
- AR-V7: not yet tested at this visit

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY
- Hypertension (newly diagnosed on enzalutamide, managed with amlodipine 5 mg daily)
- Former smoker, 30 pack-years, quit age 55
- Osteoporosis risk given prolonged ADT; on bone-protective therapy

SOCIAL HISTORY
Retired civil engineer. Lives with wife. Two adult children involved in care. French-Canadian descent. Former smoker (quit ~16 years ago). Non-drinker. Ambulatory at home with some deconditioning.

FAMILY HISTORY
Father: prostate cancer, died age 78. Paternal aunt: breast cancer age 62. Germline BRCA2 identified in proband; family members advised to pursue cascade genetic testing.

ALLERGIES
No known drug allergies.

MEDICATIONS
1. Leuprolide acetate 22.5 mg IM q3 months (last dose on file)
2. Olaparib (Lynparza) 300 mg PO BID
3. Amlodipine 5 mg PO daily (hypertension)
4. Zoledronic acid (Zometa) 4 mg IV q3 months (last dose on file)
5. Calcium carbonate 1200 mg PO daily
6. Vitamin D3 1000 IU PO daily
7. Acetaminophen 650 mg PO q6h scheduled (back pain)
8. Ondansetron 4 mg PO PRN nausea
9. Iron sulfate 325 mg PO daily (started at prior visit based on anticipatory guidance)

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
General: Alert, pleasant, appears mildly fatigued and somewhat pale. In no acute distress.
Vitals: BP 138/82 mmHg, HR 94 bpm, RR 16, SpO2 97% on room air, Weight 90 kg, Temp 36.7°C.
ECOG PS: 1
Skin/Conjunctiva: Pallor of conjunctivae and nail beds noted. No jaundice. No petechiae or ecchymoses.
HEENT: No oral lesions or mucositis. Dry mucous membranes, mild.
Lymph nodes: No cervical, supraclavicular, or axillary lymphadenopathy palpated.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs. Mild tachycardia at rest (HR 94).
Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes or crackles.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly appreciated. No palpable mass.
Musculoskeletal: Midline thoracic tenderness to palpation over T10-T12 region, improved from last visit. No focal neurologic deficits. No lower extremity edema today (improved from earlier in treatment course).
Neurologic: Cranial nerves grossly intact. Gait steady, improved from enzalutamide-era dizziness.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS
PSA: 3.1 ng/mL (prior baseline at olaparib start: 9.2 ng/mL → 66% decline)
Testosterone: castrate at 10 ng/dL
CBC:
  - Hemoglobin: 9.6 g/dL (LOW; was 11.8 at olaparib initiation, 8-week decline of 2.2 g/dL)
  - Hematocrit: 29.1%
  - MCV: 85 fL (normocytic)
  - WBC: 5.8 x10^9/L (within normal limits)
  - ANC: 3.1 x10^9/L
  - Platelets: 162 x10^9/L (mildly decreased from 198 at baseline)
  - Reticulocyte count: pending
Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
  - Creatinine: 1.1 mg/dL; eGFR ~64 mL/min (stable, CKD stage 2)
  - ALT: 34 U/L, AST: 28 U/L, Alkaline phosphatase: 194 U/L (mildly elevated, likely bony)
  - Bilirubin total: 0.7 mg/dL
  - Albumin: 3.6 g/dL
LDH: 198 U/L (mildly elevated, within range for metastatic disease)
Iron studies: Ferritin 86 ng/mL, TIBC 340 mcg/dL, serum iron 72 mcg/dL — transferrin saturation ~21% (borderline low)

---

IMAGING RESULTS
No new imaging obtained at this visit. Most recent imaging: bone scan at enzalutamide progression (T10, T12, right 4th rib new lesions). Spine MRI confirming T10 epidural extension (mod canal narrowing, no cord signal change). Palliative radiation T10-T12 completed. CT chest/abdomen/pelvis not yet re-staged on olaparib; planned after 4 months per institutional protocol. PSA trajectory alone is reassuring for early treatment response.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. CASTRATION-RESISTANT PROSTATE CANCER WITH GERMLINE BRCA2 MUTATION, ON OLAPARIB
Excellent early PSA response — PSA down 66% from 9.2 to 3.1 ng/mL at 8 weeks. This is consistent with the expected kinetics of PARP inhibitor response in HRR-deficient mCRPC per PROfound trial data. Testosterone confirmed castrate. Clinically, patient reports subjective improvement in bone pain. Olaparib ongoing at full dose (300 mg BID). Continue current regimen. Restaging CT planned at approximately 4-month mark.

2. ANEMIA — OLAPARIB-RELATED, GRADE 2
Hemoglobin has declined from 11.8 → 9.6 g/dL over 8 weeks. Patient is symptomatic with fatigue and mild exertional dyspnea. This is a recognized hematologic toxicity of olaparib, attributable to PARP inhibitor-mediated suppression of hematopoietic progenitor repair. Iron deficiency partially contributory given borderline transferrin saturation; oral iron supplementation already initiated. Hemoglobin 9.6 g/dL does not yet meet grade 3 threshold (< 8.0), though clinical symptoms are notable. No evidence of hemolysis (LDH, bilirubin unremarkable; hemolysis panel pending). No bleeding source identified.
   - Plan: Continue olaparib at current dose for now given good oncologic response
   - Increase iron supplementation; consider IV iron (ferric carboxymaltose) if oral not adequate
   - Monitor CBC weekly for the next 4 weeks, then every 2 weeks
   - Transfusion threshold: if Hgb drops below 8.0 g/dL or patient becomes more symptomatic, transfuse pRBC
   - Nutritional assessment with dietitian — adequate protein and caloric intake
   - If Hgb drops to grade 3 level, hold olaparib and consider dose reduction to 200 mg BID per prescribing guidelines

3. NAUSEA — GRADE 1, OLAPARIB-RELATED
Well-controlled with ondansetron PRN. Taking olaparib with food; patient counseled to continue this strategy. No dose modification needed at this time.

4. BONE-PROTECTIVE THERAPY / VERTEBRAL METASTASES
Zoledronic acid continued quarterly. Back pain well-controlled with scheduled acetaminophen post-radiation. No new neurologic concerns; T10 epidural disease being monitored — patient to report immediately any lower extremity weakness, numbness, or changes in bladder/bowel function. No intervention needed at this visit.

5. HYPERTENSION
BP 138/82 mmHg on amlodipine 5 mg, adequate control. Continue current management.

6. GENERAL SUPPORTIVE CARE
Palliative care team engaged. Patient and wife involved in goals-of-care discussions (initiated at prior visit). ECOG PS 1. Patient ambulating, living at home with good family support.

Return to clinic in 3-4 weeks for repeat CBC and symptom check. Sooner if hemoglobin drops further, new pain, or neurologic symptoms develop. PSA recheck in 6 weeks. CT restaging planned at 4 months on olaparib.

---
Electronically attested by: GU Oncology
Dictation reviewed and approved.
